item.title: Junoir Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image could not be reliably sourced, the closest result being a deleted DA art page made by user Rhetoricism titled Acceptably Beige. As such, it is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0 and has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the concept but the writing loses it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It seems too convenient that:

"the backstage area cannot be entered or observed by any means"

How does the Foundation know this? It seems like this is true only because you tell us so, which is straight up lazy.

Downvote.

EDIT: In summary: On this aspect of the skip, I need more than a sentence in order to suspend my disbelief.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel that blackouts and redactions in the containment procedures are an immediate downvote. This is meant to allow staff to know what to do in an emergency/outbreak and not knowing which site to contact is a problem.

Edit. I thought about this and I am being a little too presciptive. Not always reason for a downvote, so no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed!
Sorry about that, I haven't been active on the site for a damnably long time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On ██/██/██, an attempt was made by Agent ██████ to recover Dr. ████ from SCP-1743 with .

Now I know that's not what you meant to say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Main article felt very poorly written, had to reread multiple times to get what you were trying to say, and the effect still feels muddy and not fully fleshed out. The log was narmy and short, didn't win me over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd like to see logs with more variations.

We know what happens if a second person tries to interfere. But what happens if two people go on simultaneously? Are they co-stars? Is one the main character and the other is forced to be the "wacky neighbor" in what he believes is his real life? That could be pretty nightmarish.

What if somebody who knows the effects goes on stage? No matter how convincing the illusion otherwise, I think a subject with prior knowledge would quickly realize that they were under the effect when the laugh track kicks in.
For myself, I'd quit participating, just sit in one place and not do anything that might be entertaining to the hypothetical audience. Maybe they would get bored and the "studio execs" would cancel the show early so I can leave.

Lots of interesting possiblities with the workings of this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. I found the concept poorly defined in the article, though I picked up "deadly sitcom" by the end, but the log is illuminative and interesting. I'd actually like to see more of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like, and would enjoy seeing more logs. +1 good sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have a pretty solid point, sir. Ergo, I have added in a purpose for these terrible abominations!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright, just made a shitton of improvements to this. I thank you all for your criticisms, and I welcome any more!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Welp, that's another lurker down the drain.

We seem to use em up faster than D-class around here…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You thought you said what before?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My understanding was that the object made the subject's life miserable for laughs. It wouldn't be their normal life, it would be their life where everything goes wrong, and we get to laugh at it. Come to think of it, this would be very popular in the US.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought I said this before. Really, only the log is saving you for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a verb now! 8D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, wait, don't go in the hole! Oh blast we lost another lurker… Got a shiny set of silverware out of it though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ahh, thank you so much! Everyone is talking about the tone, and this is exactly what I needed to fix it!

Edit: Fixed all of this up as best as I could. Thanks again for this, I needed it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, my apologies. I shall remember that from here on out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(peeps out of relative lurkerdom) Well said.

The laugh-track bit is the part I find most interesting. I actually would love the idea of an SCP item that causes you to have a laugh track, background music, etc., in your real life, either only heard by you or heard by many around you, for a limited time or however long the item is in your possession, etc. Is there such a beast?

(goes back into lurker hole)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm really jaded about faceless people, and that element feels tacked on here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you are not going to add any Attribute tags (as defined in the Tag Guide), then please leave the tag line blank so that others can tag it. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
People have noted the tone issues above, and I second the concerns raised there. Conceptually, I'm having issues with this. So people get pulled onto the stage and live their normal lives, except with a mean laugh track? It doesn't seem like the SCP goes out of its way to kill people, but that's what seems to be happening in a very short time period. I guess because of the laugh track?

I see two concepts going on here; malicious laugh track, and creepy faceless people. The first I can see working in some manner (it reminds me of the "Sideways House" thing from The State); the second is very creepy, but you'll be very hard pressed to do anything with it that doesn't evoke Slenderman, fair or not. I don't really see an inherent connection between the two.

I don't like the compulsion element. At this stage in the site's development, I feel like that unless the object really needs something like that, things what compel you to do stuff are lazy writing. I also don't care for the "unobservable backstage" thing. It's impossible to look back there? Like, there's some sort of crazy, inpenetrable force field around it? We can't x-ray it? We can't poke a hole in a wall somewhere? I would prefer the backstage appearing normal when it's observed, but people who get hauled into it disappear or something.

This isn't terrible, but there's too much wrong in the execution, and the concept feels kind of weirdly mashed together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
People have been talking about tone, and because I want this to succeed, I'm Soulesshuman-ing this where I see tone issues.

+ show block
– hide block

the sort usually used for a studio audience television program.

Eh on the phrasing. Be careful with comparisons in clinical reports.

hose employed at ██████ Studio Center similarly have no memory of how it came to be

Take out similarly.

which tends to happen due to a "luring" effect

I've said this before, but I dislike when people use quotes as an excuse to break tone when there is no reference to the individual who said quoted line. The rest of the paragraph needs trimming. I understand on what you want to get across to the reader, but be more subtle about it. If I were to rewrite this:

SCP-1743 enters its active state when a human subject walks onto its main stage. The main stage can only be entered from the viewing area; areas backstage are not accessible through any means. After entering SCP-1743, the subject will not be able to perceive the object or any off-stage activity, instead perceiving that they are going about their daily lives. To date, it remains impossible to remove the subject from the stage through physical means or otherwise. (See Addendum 1743-A) [I'm not happy about this rewrite, but it's not up to me to write it.]

I would then go on to explain the effects of the object, instead of combining the activation procedure with the effects. I left out the 'luring' effect, mainly because it's cheesy and way too many objects rely on this to be horrifying.

which appears to be a standard television studio camera

I think it is a standard television studio camera. It's not a laser-shooting marmoset that appears to be a television camera.

Rewrite: SCP-1743-2 are humanoids that appear onstage during SCP-1743's active phase. These beings appear to look and act like people the subject would encounter in it's day-to-day life. However, instances of SCP-1743-2 are without defining facial characteristics such as eyes or a mouth. Subjects under the influence of SCP-1743 do not appear to be aware of the difference in facial structure, and converse with the objects normally.


I don't have too many issues with the rest of the article. My rewrites are bad, and I wouldn't use them, but I was trying to demonstrate how to condense the information and avoid being too wordy. I still think this should get a chance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What Boa said. It needs to be less narrative-sounding. Passive voice is your friend. Maybe I'm just reading it wrong, but the concept is a bit unclear for me. It's a soundstage that when you go on it, you live out an evil sitcom version of your life with an ensemble cast led by Slenderman? I mean, I'd totally watch it, but as an SCP, it doesn't work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the oddest tone situation I can remember having enountered. The phrasing comes close to being appropriate but diverges just enough to be jarring. I think we've discovered the SCP-article equivalent of the uncanny valley…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This isn't bad, but i think it needs a less lengthy containment description, and more details on the effects on the victim. Do they expire from natural causes (starvation, dehydration) or does the stage cause accidents like in the video log? If the former, do the subjects notice their declining health? What if someone somehow realizes they are trapped in an illusion? Maybe also go more into the luring effect, far too many SCPs have one that is not detailed in any way, just "subjects really want to hold it/walk in/read/look at it". Withholding vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm really close to upvoting this, but I think it needs some tone-fixes and polish first.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this a lot; I like the idea itself, and the execution isn't bad either. You have my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Here's hoping this isn't too close to being the Game Show of Death.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tone feels off, the writing is a little clunky in some places, definitely needs a good slather of polish. All of that aside, the idea just doesn't click for me and I personally doubt it ever will.
